latest.title: Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
latest.content:
26 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1669Y
dotDigital Group plc
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, is pleased to announce the appointment of Mike Harris as Chief Revenue Officer (non-board position), effective 1 April 2026.
The Chief Revenue Officer role is a newly created Group position, established to bring all global go
‑
to
‑
market functions together under unified leadership as the Group continues to scale internationally. The role reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer and is responsible for leading Dotdigital's global commercial organisation and revenue performance, with a focus on driving more consistent execution across regions and improving expansion, retention and customer lifetime value.
Mike has served as Dotdigital's Interim GM for EMEA since July 2025, during which time he has made a strong contribution to the Group's commercial strategy and revenue performance in the region.
Mike brings extensive senior leadership experience in scaling revenue across global B2B SaaS and marketing technology businesses. Most recently, he served as CRO at Haiilo, where he drove several growth, retention and efficiency-related initiatives. Prior to this, he held senior commercial leadership roles including Vice President of Global Sales at parcelLab and Senior Vice-President of Global Sales at Monetate, both marketing technology platforms. Mike has also held senior roles at Bluecore and Omniture.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"Dotdigital has strengthened significantly in recent years through sustained product investment and targeted acquisitions, building a broader, more capable platform. We will continue to invest as we extend that advantage, while at the same time focusing on raising the consistency and pace of execution globally. Creating the Chief Revenue Officer role gives us clear leadership of the Group's commercial engine and reinforces our ambition to scale internationally with discipline.
"Since joining us as interim GM for EMEA last summer, Mike has raised the bar on focus, pace and commercial discipline. I am delighted he will now lead this agenda as Chief Revenue Officer as we deepen customer success, drive expansion and retention, and deliver sustainable, high-quality recurring revenue growth."
Mike Harris, incoming CRO, commented:
"Over the past several months I have seen a platform with real strength, a loyal customer base, and a team that cares deeply about what we do - and we know that our success is ultimately defined by the success of our customers. As CRO, my priority is to help our teams focus on the right opportunities, make performance visible through clear metrics, and support customers to realise value faster. I am excited to take on the role and help drive the next stage of growth."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO

Tom Mullan, CFO

About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of cross-channel marketing automation technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's customer experience and data platform (CXDP) combines the power of automation and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 4,000 brands across 150 countries.

company_30s.what_they_do: DOTD.L is a UK‑based marketing‑automation platform that helps brands create, send and analyse email, SMS and social‑media campaigns. It offers data‑driven personalization, customer‑journey mapping and integration with e‑commerce systems.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global demand for automated, cross‑channel marketing is growing fast as businesses shift from manual outreach to AI‑powered, real‑time engagement. DOTD’s tools compete with big names like HubSpot and Marketo but focus on mid‑market customers that need flexible, cost‑effective solutions.
company_30s.current_state: DOTD is in a transition phase: it just finished an acquisition of a complementary data‑analytics firm (Alia), has appointed a new Chief Revenue Officer to drive sales, and is reporting its latest interim results. The company is expanding its product line and seeking new revenue streams, but it remains in a tight cash‑flow window and its share price sits far below its historical highs.

rns_narrative: DOTD has been busy with leadership changes—new CRO and director share‑holding updates—indicating a shift toward aggressive growth.
The acquisition of Alia signals a strategic pivot to strengthen data capabilities and broaden the product suite.
Recent interim results and shareholder filings show the company is tightening operations while preparing for a larger sales push.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent market, waiting for a catalyst
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are very few bullish voices, but some traders see the acquisition and new CRO as a sign that DOTD is ready to capture a larger slice of the marketing‑automation market.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most chatter is skeptical: the company’s valuation is high relative to its earnings, integration of Alia could be costly, and the market may not reward the expansion.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral to mildly bearish, but overall engagement is low—no one is actively pushing the stock up or down.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the market is not watching DOTD closely and a surprise catalyst could trigger a big move.

near_term_catalysts: The first quarter earnings report could show revenue growth from Alia’s customers, providing a positive earnings surprise.
A public announcement of a new partnership or integration with a major e‑commerce platform would lift demand for DOTD’s services.
A volume spike above the 10‑day average paired with a breakout above the recent low would signal the start of a recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals over the next 2–3 months are: (1) volume exceeding 2× the 10‑day average, (2) a price bounce to the 50‑day moving average, (3) a positive earnings surprise of at least 10%, and (4) any announcement that the Alia data engine is fully integrated and driving sales.
expect_next.timing_regime: DOTD sits in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle. The 86% drawdown suggests capitulation is almost complete, and the APEX score indicates a tight compression. Confirmation of mean reversion will likely come from a volume‑driven breakout or a clear earnings beat.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down 86%, a depth that historically yields a 162% upside when a bounce occurs. The new CRO and Alia acquisition create a realistic catalyst for revenue acceleration. If the price rebounds to the midpoint of its previous high, the upside is roughly 1.6× the current level, giving a favorable risk‑reward profile.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Integration risk, potential dilution from new share issuances, and the competitive pressure in the marketing‑automation space could blunt the rebound. If the company fails to meet earnings expectations, the price could fall another 10–15% from the current low.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Maximum loss is capped at the 86% drop (the current level is already that far below the peak). Potential gain is about 162% if the bounce hits the midpoint of the prior high, giving a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1.9:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a clear volume‑driven breakout above the recent low, with a stop 20% below that level. Allocate 5–10% of the portfolio per trade and trail the stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either (a) earnings beat by >10% or (b) a partnership announcement. If either occurs and volume spikes, consider a small position to test the move.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider abandoning the play if the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or the market announces a major regulatory hit that could wipe out the valuation.

ai_insights.bottom_line: DOTD sits at an 86% crash, new leadership plus an acquisition offers a realistic catalyst, and the upside could be 162%—a high‑asymmetry contrarian play that only needs a volume‑driven breakout to get going.

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 90% upside. Current position: +17.3%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
